Division of Rare and Manuscript Collections

DESCRIPTIVE SUMMARY

Title:
Media Services records, [ca. 1950-2016].
Repository:
Division of Rare and Manuscript Collections
Collection Number:
21-36-2566
Abstract:
Film prints (sole surviving copies of very old films) and film printing materials (for which there is no film print or video copy) including films on topics in agriculture, home economics, and general Cornell topics. Also, audio recordings, slide sets and inventories and other items produced by Media Services and Technology Group.
Creator:
Cornell University. Media Services
Cornell Information Technologies. Distributed Learning Services
Cornell University. Media Services. Distribution Center
Quanitities:
111.1 cubic feet.
3800 videocassettes.
71 DVDs.
77 CDs.
26 audiocassettes.
Language:
Collection material in English
ORGANIZATIONAL HISTORY

In 2003, the Educational Television Center from Media and Technology Services joined CIT. The Educational Television Center provides video production services ranging from basic professional recordings to public television documentary and entertainment programs. It also houses Cornell's satellite television services. Its studio facility has been the point of origin for numerous live national satellite video conferences and the venue for Cornell faculty and area experts appearing on network television. The Cornell Information Technologies Integrated Audio and Video Engineering group, which formed from Classroom Technologies and Video Collaborative Services, inherited some of the tape archive as well as the functions of the Media Services Group, though they technically do not organizationally descend from Media Services.
COLLECTION DESCRIPTION

Audio visual materials produced by Media Services and the Technology Services Group. Film prints (sole surviving copies of very old films) and film printing materials (for which there is no film print or video copy) including movie films on topics in agriculture, home economics, and general Cornell topics. Also, audio recordings, slide sets, videotapes,and inventories. videotapes, audiotapes, CDs, DVDs, etc. produced by the Media Services and Technology Group. Projects include videos about the Cornell Chimes/McGraw Tower restoration, Cornell's 9/11 "Day of Remembrance, September 14, 2001. Cornell Honors the Victims of the September 11th Terrorist Attacks on America" (V-1451) and the university's annual Commencement, all distributed worldwide. Collection also contains digital files created by the Cornell Information Technologies Integrated Audio and Video Engineering group.
RELATED MATERIALS

See also Media Services Publications, #21-36-2729
INFORMATION FOR USERS
Cite As:

Media Services records, #21-36-2566. Division of Rare and Manuscript Collections, Cornell University Library.
Conditions Governing Use

Audio visual media that has been digitized: V-979, V-2336, V-2398 to V-2401, V-4437, V-6884 to V-6907, V-6909 to V-6912, V-11120, V-11198, V-11202 to V-11205, V-11214, V-11216, V-11218, V-11223, V-11230 to V-11236, V-11244 to V-11252, V-11255, V-11256.
Conditions Governing Use

Due to the nature of electronic records, use of all born digital content on physical media such as CDs, DVDs, floppy disks, zip disks, and external hard drives is limited to digital copies.
Conditions Governing Use

Due to the fragility and potential degradation of moving image and sound recordings, viewing and listening is limited to items that have been digitized. If an item is in another media format, you may request to have the item digitized for access. Information on ordering access copies may be found on our Reproductions & Permissions page.

Box 21-36-2566|22 F-859
"Four Nursery Schools" Copy 1 - Cornell Film. (Use DVD-1003 in same box)
Approx 30mins
Scope and Contents
Click here to see a digitized version of the film.
This film examines four nursery school environments. The first is the nursery school at Cornell University that is part of the College of Home Economics, the second is the nursery school for children with special needs at St. Christopher's Hospital for Children, part of Temple University's School of Medicine, the third is the School of Home Economics Children's Center at Syracuse University and forth is the Philadelphia Experimental Nursery School Program. Project participants include Clara P. Baldwin, Helen T. M. Bayer, and Laurel Hodgkin.

"Two Ball Games" - Cornell Film (2 copes). (Use DVD-1000 in same box)
 1975
Approx 30mins
Scope and Contents
Click here to see a digitized version of the film.
Captures the difference between two baseball games being played by children. One is a informal game in a park in Fall Creek, Ithaca and the other is a formal Little League game in Auburn, New York. Produced by the College of Human Ecology and the Department of Psychology.

Box 21-36-2566|24 F-869
Home Economics in the Modern University - Cornell Film
Scope and Contents
Click here to see a digitized version of the film.
Promotional film made in the mid-1960s for the College of Home Economics, now Human Ecology. Reviews life at Cornell University and courses of study within the College of Home Economics such as nutrition, design and child development. The film also discusses how a degree in Home Economics can lead to a professional career.

"Learning While They Play" - Cornell Film. (Use DVD-1002 in same box)
 1965
Scope and Contents
Click here to see a digitized version of the film.
Discusses the importance of play on children's educational development. Project participants include Clara Baldwin and Dr. Helen T.M. Bayer. Produced by the College of Home Economics, Human Ecology.
Box 21-36-2566|20 DVD-1002
"Learning While They Play" - copy of F-839
 1965
Box 21-36-2566|20 F-840
"Measuring Up: Some Methods Employed in Nutrition Research" - Cornell Film. (Use DVD-999 in same box)
 1966
Scope and Contents
Click here to see a digitized version of the film.
Discusses the research being done in the nutritional sciences including a study on how to measure the amount of body weight attributable to fat, the effects of vitamin B6 on the body for use in establishing national nutritional standards and the role calcium plays in the development of osteoporosis. Project participants include Professor Charlotte Young and Dr. Leo Luprock. Produced by the College of Home Economics, now Human Ecology.
Box 21-36-2566|20 DVD-999
"Measuring Up" - Copy of F-840
 1966
Box 21-36-2566|20 F-842
"Nutrition Sense and Nonsense" - Cornell Film. (Use DVD-997 in same box)
 1962
Scope and Contents
Click here to see a digitized version of the film.
A consumer economics film advising people to be aware of modern "magic" remedies and diet fads that advertise quick and dramatic results.

Box 21-36-2566|31 V-6884
"The American Family: Who Cares?" (Use DVD-979 in same box)
 1976
Scope and Contents
Click here to see a digitized version of the video.
A taped lecture of Professor Urie Bronfenbrenner discussing the break-up of the traditional American family, the general state of isolation and the impact on children and society.
